characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Abusive Parents: Par for the course with this team. D'avin and John's father was by all accounts a complete asshole to them and their mother; in fact, D'avin's favorite memory from their childhood is the time their father disappeared for a month. Dutch was raised in a harem and had her childhood further ruined by Khlyen, who trained her to be an assassin and murdered her husband on her wedding night. Badass Crew: A team of Levels 3 (later Level 5), 4, & 5 Reclamation Agents, all of whom are exceedingly badass. Big Damn Heroes: They all play this role at one point or another.In the first episode, Dutch recovers from a near fatal dose of poison and turns up just in the nick of time to save the boys when they get pinned down by some guards and are running low on ammo. It'sglorious.John ends up playing this role more often than most would expect: as the tech guy, he's usually off somewhere by himself whenever trouble goes down on a mission, which leaves him free to come to the rescue when necessary. In Season 3, he actually tells Zeph that it's part of their job description as the team nerds."The thing about nerds is that we save the day way more than people think. So let's do some nerdy shit and save the day." In the first episode, Dutch recovers from a near fatal dose of poison and turns up just in the nick of time to save the boys when they get pinned down by some guards and are running low on ammo. It'sglorious. John ends up playing this role more often than most would expect: as the tech guy, he's usually off somewhere by himself whenever trouble goes down on a mission, which leaves him free to come to the rescue when necessary. In Season 3, he actually tells Zeph that it's part of their job description as the team nerds."The thing about nerds is that we save the day way more than people think. So let's do some nerdy shit and save the day."

characters.name: Team Awesome Force
characters.description: Abusive Parents: Par for the course with this team. D'avin and John's father was by all accounts a complete asshole to them and their mother; in fact, D'avin's favorite memory from their childhood is the time their father disappeared for a month. Dutch was raised in a harem and had her childhood further ruined by Khlyen, who trained her to be an assassin and murdered her husband on her wedding night. Badass Crew: A team of Levels 3 (later Level 5), 4, & 5 Reclamation Agents, all of whom are exceedingly badass. Big Damn Heroes: They all play this role at one point or another.In the first episode, Dutch recovers from a near fatal dose of poison and turns up just in the nick of time to save the boys when they get pinned down by some guards and are running low on ammo. It'sglorious.John ends up playing this role more often than most would expect: as the tech guy, he's usually off somewhere by himself whenever trouble goes down on a mission, which leaves him free to come to the rescue when necessary. In Season 3, he actually tells Zeph that it's part of their job description as the team nerds."The thing about nerds is that we save the day way more than people think. So let's do some nerdy shit and save the day." In the first episode, Dutch recovers from a near fatal dose of poison and turns up just in the nick of time to save the boys when they get pinned down by some guards and are running low on ammo. It'sglorious. John ends up playing this role more often than most would expect: as the tech guy, he's usually off somewhere by himself whenever trouble goes down on a mission, which leaves him free to come to the rescue when necessary. In Season 3, he actually tells Zeph that it's part of their job description as the team nerds."The thing about nerds is that we save the day way more than people think. So let's do some nerdy shit and save the day."

characters.name: Dutch
characters.description: A Level Five Reclamation Agent, and the leader of the team. Action Girl: Kicks as much ass, if not more, than the boys. Affectionate Nickname: Khlyen and Aneela call her Yala or "little bird." Ambiguously Bi: She's usually shown with male lovers, but her interactions with Delle Seyah are dripping with subtext that suggests she's also attracted to women. Possibly less ambiguous: she is essentially Aneela allowed to grow up differently, and Aneela gets together with Delle Seyah in Season 3. Amnesiac Lover:To D'avin in the Season 5 opener.

characters.name: John Jaqobis
characters.description: Level Three, later Level Five Reclamation Agent, and second-in-command to Dutch. Ace Pilot: John is a "lover of ships" and is easily the best pilot of the team."Lady, I can fly anything." Alliterative Name:JohnJaqobis, with extra points for his actor beingAaronAshmore. Always Someone Better: He's got issues with his older brother. D'avin getting fast-tracked to Level 4, which means he's technically superior to John, does not help. Ambiguously Bi: He's only ever been shown with female lovers, but there are a few moments that suggest he's also attracted to guys, and a subtle running gag indicates that he's into vibrators and dildos.

characters.name: D'avin Jaqobis
characters.description: John's older brother, an ex-soldier with a troubled past. Ambiguously Bi: He's only been shown with female lovers, but his grudging friendships with Alvis and Fancy contain a lot of what can only be described as hate-flirting. Amnesiac Lover:To Dutch in the Season 5 opener. The Atoner: In Season 1, he feels enormous guilt over killing his squad while in the military — exacerbated by the fact that he can't rememberwhyit happened, only what he did — and his search for answers drives his initial plotline. This begins to fade in Season 2, indicating that learning it was the result of a mind control experiment and that he wasn't in control of what happened has given him a little peace. Badass in Distress: He's captured by Khlyen at the end of Season 1, and the beginning of Season 2 has Dutch and John doing everything they can to find and rescue him.

characters.name: Lucy
characters.description: The AI on Dutch and John's ship. Ambiguously Bi: About as much of this trope as a ship's AI can manage. Lucy has a thing for John (one of the last things she does with her borrowed gynoid body is to kiss him) and she also finds Clara attractive. For a human. A.I. Is a Crapshoot: It's not clear if she's actually sentient; John does treat her as such, though nobody else does. Heroic Sacrifice: She asks John topurge her from the ship as she'd been corrupted by the Lady and was putting Team Awesome Force at risk. Robo Ship:In-Universewith John to the point that she prioritizes his life over Dutch's.Dutch:(Stuck in quarantine as nanomachines eat her alive)"Lucy, I need to blow up that ship before these things tear me a new one!"Lucy:I'm sorry, but quarantine protocols state you must remain in airlock one full hour before—Dutch:Lucy, John's in trouble!(Beat, then the airlock door slides open)Dutch:Favourites, my arse! I'm taking over, you mechanical bitch!Also, when she gets herself an gynoid body in the episode "I Love Lucy", one of the last things she does with it is kiss John passionately.Let's also not forget that Lucy is literally a robot ship.

characters.name: Pree
characters.description: Proprietor of The Royale, a local bar in Old Town. '80s Hair: Used to have this in the past. He brings it back briefly in Season 3 and it isglorious. Seriously, dude looks like he belongs in a hair metal band. Beware the Nice Ones: He's a friendly, jovial guy, but that doesn't mean he won't stab a guy in the hand for trying to take over his bar.Pree:Do not try me on for size becausethis bitch be deputized! Camp Gay: He'sfabulous. Expansion Pack Past: He has a colorful and illicit past but doesn't like to volunteer information.He used to be a career criminal known as "Big Daddy Scrimshaw" who specialized in the theft and trade of dangerous weapons, which catches Dutch and John completely off guard.Pree: "What, none of you have skeletons?"He also used to be a warlord by the name of "Varrick", and the Company still considers him to be a terrorist.

characters.name: Alvis Akari
characters.description: Old Town monk, and secretly a leader in an underground Westerlyn revolutionary movement. Badass Preacher: He's a monk, and is also one of the leaders of a revolutionary movement. Character Death: He is murdered by Aneela in the penultimate episode of Season 3. Crisis of Faith: Has one of these after he tries to kill everyone living in a Company outpost with a chemical weapon. It doesn't last. Deadpan Snarker: You wouldn't know it, but the mild-mannered self-mutilating monk is one of the snarkiest characters in thesetting.

characters.name: Illenore Pawter Simms
characters.description: A RAC certified doctor who lives in Old Town. And I Must Scream: While she and John are under the influence of thewall the Company built around Old Town as a giant pacifier to keep the populace both happy and completely helpless. While everybody around them is in agony (one individual had fallen from a height high enough that his lower half is mush, for example) they both feel like a million bucks, seemingly too high to even notice...except for the tears running down their faces. Affectionate Nickname: Hills calls her "Red" because of her hair. Back-Alley Doctor: Even though she is RAC certified, her practice has many elements of this trope and she plays fast and loose with the rules. Badass Boast: In "Full Metal Monk":Pawter:I'm not your hostage anymore, Jelco. I'm your liege. Deny me again, and I will use the full strength of Land Simms to step on you like the sycophantic bug that you are.

characters.name: Hills Oonan
characters.description: Chief of Old Town security, and main enforcer of Company law. The Alcoholic: The stress between his job and his conscience has made him into this. Back for the Dead: He reappears in "Wild, Wild Westerley" and dies near the end of the episode. Boom, Headshot!: Jelco shoots him in the head for warning people about the bombing. Category Traitor: As a native Westerlyn who does the Company's dirty work, he's seen this way by a lot of people, including Alvis.

characters.name: Sabine
characters.description: The new bartender at The Royale, after the bombing of Old Town. Action Girl: Once her real identity is revealed, she proves to be highly lethal in hand to hand combat. Artifact Alias: Her real name, Jenny Avery, is only used once, and she's always referred to as Sabine. Boom, Headshot!: How Dutch kills her. Brought Down to Badass: Dutch remarks that D'avin temporarily "knocked the superpowers out of [her]." Even without them, she's still dangerous, and it's because of this that Dutch is hesitant to trust her.

characters.name: Gared
characters.description: Old Town thug with an unfortunate habit of picking fights with the wrong people. Becomes Pree's boyfriend by Season 3, and later his husband. Affectionate Nickname: Gare-Bear. Rhymes with "Care Bear." Beware the Nice Ones: He might be a pacifist and one of the nicest guys in Westerley, but threaten his man and he won't care if you're Hullen, he willendyou. The Big Guy: And he's big enough thatDutch, of all people, has a briefThis Is Gonna Suckmoment when she fights him. Butt-Monkey: Gets his leg broken by Dutch, gets the same leg broken some more by Pawter and then gets stabbed in the hand by Pree. All in the same episode.

characters.name: Weymer Simms
characters.description: Pawter's father, a Qreshi carpenter who married up. Cruel to Be Kind: It's revealed that he didn't oppose Pawter's exile - and in factproposedit - because he didn't want his daughter to grow up in a world where she had to be a Machiavellian schemer to survive. Good Parents: In contrast to the cold and stern Adaline, he is kind and understanding. Heroic Sacrifice: Gives his life exposing himself to infection and later manually detonating the bomb to help Johnny destroy the bioweapon. Uptown Girl: He was the Downtown Boy to Adaline... relatively. He was still a carpenter on Qresh, which is definitely quite hoity toity compared to Westerley.

characters.name: Louella Simms
characters.description: Pawter's younger sister, and later head of Land Simms. An Arm and a Leg: Pawter chops off her arm before the infection she gets from touching the fog can spread. Broken Bird: In the space of one episode, she loses her arm, her parents and her fiance, the latter of which was actually the cause of her other losses. Four episodes later, she loses her sister and is denied revenge by the rest of the Nine. Chekhov's Skill: She's taken up deep-sea diving. She doesn't do any diving in the episode she first appears in, but it does mean she owns a diving suit. Now, how useful would a diving suit be in the middle of a bioweapon attack? Turns out very. The Glorious War of Sisterly Rivalry: The quiet, obedient one to Pawter's rebelliousFallen Princess.

characters.name: Liam Jelco
characters.description: A Company Officer assigned to Old Town after the bombing, to restore order to what remains. Later on, it's revealed that he is also tasked with overseeing the transition of Old Town residents into Hullen. Amazon Chaser: He seems to have a thing for powerful women.In "Wild, Wild Westerley," he looks approvingly at Dutch after she escapes being tied up, and his tone implies he's flirting with her.Several scenes imply he has aVillainous Crushon Pawter, in spite of (or maybe because of) her resisting his advances. There's theGo-Go Enslavementmoment at the end of "Wild, Wild Westerley," and an episode later he's bringing her flowers and inviting her to dinner. When she intimidates him into letting Johnny out of detainment in "Full Metal Monk," he looks more aroused than annoyed. In the same episode, helicks her neckwhile she's under the effects of the containment fence.Jelco:Power looks good on you, Seyah.He's also attracted to Borna when he meets her in "Johnny Be Good." By the third season, he's married to her, and says that he loves her because she is "powerful and wily". In "Wild, Wild Westerley," he looks approvingly at Dutch after she escapes being tied up, and his tone implies he's flirting with her. Several scenes imply he has aVillainous Crushon Pawter, in spite of (or maybe because of) her resisting his advances. There's theGo-Go Enslavementmoment at the end of "Wild, Wild Westerley," and an episode later he's bringing her flowers and inviting her to dinner. When she intimidates him into letting Johnny out of detainment in "Full Metal Monk," he looks more aroused than annoyed. In the same episode, helicks her neckwhile she's under the effects of the containment fence.Jelco:Power looks good on you, Seyah. He's also attracted to Borna when he meets her in "Johnny Be Good." By the third season, he's married to her, and says that he loves her because she is "powerful and wily".

characters.name: Pippin Foster
characters.description: Pippin Foster, aka "the Mouth", is the spoiled youngest son of a mid-rank family on Qresh. He is also an accomplished Black Market broker specializing in finding anything smuggled in and out of the Quad. Ambiguously Bi: Apparently finds D'avin attractive enough to refer to Dutch as "thegirlhot one". Brainwashed and Crazy:Briefly, after he gets one of the Lady's spiders stuck in his brain. Friend in the Black Market: Becomes this for Dutch and her crew. He has some fairly...unorthodox ways of getting them what they need. Heroic Sacrifice:He dies sabotaging the RAC ship so that it can't be retaken by the Hullen.

characters.name: Alfred Olyevich Turin
characters.description: A high ranking member of the RAC. By-the-Book Cop: He is deeply devoted to the rules and regulations of the RAC, and royally pissed by Dutch'stendency to bend them. Da Chief: When a RAC agent deviates from the rules, he is the one who will tear them a new one. Defiant to the End: Even at the mercy of his enemies, he continues to mouth off and insult. Didn't See That Coming: Khlyen as the 'ally' of Dutch stabbing him, or his arrest by his former allies.D'avin:Commander Alfred Olyevich Turin, you're under arrest for sedition and crimes against your people.

characters.name: Fancy Lee
characters.description: A Level Five Reclamation Agent, who often butts heads with our heroes. Bad Guys Do the Dirty Work: Invoked. He insists that he's the asshole because every organization needs one to do the things others won't. Brainwashed and Crazy: As a consequence of being made into aSuper-Soldier, he's been programmed to be loyal to Khlyen. Break Them by Talking: Kitaan manages to convince him that the green plasma inside him wasn't completely purged from his system. Not that it helped her much, but it did leave him with a seed of doubt. Brought Down to Badass: As a regular human being, Fancy was already a top-tier Killjoy and a genius with gadgets, and he still has these skills to fall back on after being cleansed of the plasma.

characters.name: Bellus Haardy
characters.description: Dutch's RAC contact, she has her office on the Leith Bazaar. Cool Old Lady: Dutch, at least, treats her with great respect, almost like a surrogate mom. Deadpan Snarker: Seems to be a prerequisite for a RAC agent. The Handler: Assigns the contracts and distributes the rewards. Mama Bear: She is really protective of Dutch, seeing her almost like a surrogate daughter. D'avin found out about thisthe hard way.

characters.name: Zephyr Vos
characters.description: A very-recently qualified Level 1 RAC agent who is recruited into Dutch's team to help fight the Hullen. She's one of the few characters who can operate on Johnny's level intellectually, and can even outsmart him at times. Ambiguously Bi: She's only been romantically involved with Pippin, but some of her interactions with Dutch imply she's probably also attracted to women. Awesomeness by Analysis: What was the first thing that pegged her on to the fact her first time in the field was actually a VR simulation? D'avin wasn't sweating. Dark and Troubled Past: She grew up in an agrarian community on Leith that considered science heretical. When she was fifteen, she discovered that the wheat in the granary was tainted with a poisonous fungus and burned down the granary. The village elders later tried to marry her off to her second cousin, causing her to run away. Distaff Counterpart: To John. She is definitely at least as smart as he is, though his field of expertise is technology while hers is biology.

characters.name: Joseph 'Big Joe' Siano
characters.description: Joseph 'Big Joe' Siano was one of the top RAC Agents in the Quad. Towards the end of his career he became a thief and a gun runner, which made him the target of a Black Warrant. Arms Dealer: Became this toward the end of his career with the RAC, having stolen a genetic bomb from The Company to try and sell it to a Leithian terrorist group. The Big Guy: Well they don't call the guy 'Big Joe' because he was short, do they? Friendly Sniper: He's a little gruff and rough around the edges but Joe was one of the friendliest Killjoys in the RAC. Oh, and he's also an expert sniper, too. Made of Iron: He gets kneecapped by Dutch in "The Warrior Princess Bride," and not only didhe manage to track her downin to time at all, Big Joe also spent the rest of the episode walking around with only a slight limp.

characters.name: Delle Seyah Kendry
characters.description: The head of the Kendry Family. Affably Evil: She has excellent manners and genuinely admires Dutch. She's also a cutthroat politician and executes a coup with a genetic bomb. Ambiguously Gay: She gets awfully flirty with Dutch whenever they are in the same room together. In "Escape Velocity", she greets Dutch while she's wearing nothing but her underwear, seemed disappointed that John happened to be there with her, treats Dutch more like a date than a bodyguard and even refers to her as John's sexy little partner. It becomes a lot less ambiguous when she kisses Dutch in Season 2, and then enters into a relationship with Aneela in Season 3. Ambiguously Human: By the end of Season Two she has become Hullen, thanks to Khlyen. Awesome by Analysis: She figured out that Dutch was royalty in another part of the J after seeing an instrument on her ship. Also, despite the two being exactly the same in practically every way, she can tell between Dutch and Aneela. Apparently, Aneela's eyes are prettier.

characters.name: Adaline Seyah Simms
characters.description: Head of the Simms Family and Pawter's estranged mother. Evil Matriarch: Played with. She is doing a lot of nasty things, but that seems to be par for the course for the Nine. And she's not even the worst. Hidden Depths: For all that she's got thecold patricianvibe going, she fell in love with a lower-class carpenter and fought her family into allowing her to marry him. And despite exiling her daughter she never truly lost respect for her or the hope that she'd come home and fulfill her duties. Hoist by Her Own Petard: She is killed by the same biological weapon she was developing. I Did What I Had to Do: Claims this after creating a bioweapon for use against anyone who successfully conquered Qresh. Given that the Hullen are coming to the Quad, it's hard to disagree.

characters.name: Arune Seyoh Hyponia
characters.description: Head of the Hyponia Family, and reluctant ally to Pawter. Blood from the Mouth: Starts bleeding from the mouth after being shot by Jelco. Token Good Teammate: With the exception of Pawter, the only one of the Nine we've seen so far whoisn'ta backstabbing asshole. We Hardly Knew Ye: Appears in a single episode before he's murdered by Jelco in an attempt to frame and discredit Pawter.

characters.name: Seyon Faresh
characters.description: With Delle Seyah Kendry gone, he presumably now heads the remaining Nine families. Affably Evil: He doesn't get offended when Turin unintentionally insults him, but that's probably because he wasn't planning to help. Bait the Dog: He is here to help the characters! Or not. Blue Blood: Since he's a Niner, presumably what he is. Chronic Backstabbing Disorder: This is not the first time a Niner has tried something against the rest of the planet.

characters.name: Aneela Kin Rit
characters.description: Khlyen's daughter. She is the apparent leader of the Hullen armada in their campaign to conquer the galaxy. Affectionate Nickname: Khlyen calls her "Neely." A Glass of Chianti: While not actual wine, her brief appearance in the Season 2 finale as well as some scenes in Season 3 show her drinking green plasma out of a fancy glass. Ax-Crazy: She's emotionally unstable and has an unfortunate tendency of killing those around her at the drop of a hat. Big Bad: As of Season Three. Not so much by Season Four.

characters.name: Khlyen Kin Rit
characters.description: A mysterious figure from Dutch's past. Her first mentor, who trained her as an assassin as a child, and apparently a person of some importance in the court she was raised in. Affectionate Nickname: He only ever refers to Dutch as "Yala" (a shortened version of Yalena, her true name) or as "Little Bird". He also sometimes refers to Aneela as "Neely." Ambiguously Human: The plasma changed him so that he isn't entirely human, nor does he identify himself as one. Even Evil Has Loved Ones: He genuinely loves his daughters. That doesn't mean he won't make one's life a living hell while he trains her to kill the other. Evil Mentor: The "evil" part is debatable, but deliberately creatingan assassin to kill his own daughterisn't exactly something a good person does.

characters.name: Gander
characters.description: Aneela's second in command, although he doesn't seem content with that arrangement. The Dragon: To Aneela. It eventually turns out that Gander is actually Aneela's jailor. Professional Butt-Kisser: He overdoes the sycophancy at times, which Kendry calls him out on. It's actually an act; Aneela isn't really Gander's commander, she's his prisoner. The Starscream: He's not even subtle about it.

characters.name: Kitaan
characters.description: Lucian Kitaan is a Level Six Killjoy that is captured by the RAC and is kept prisoner for information she holds about the Hullen. Ambiguously Gay: Suggests that Dutch is sweet on her at one point. Mind, she may just be messing with her. Ax-Crazy: Jesus, this chick makesAneelalook sane! Break Them by Talking: When Fancy is trying to interrogate her, Kitaan manages to plant a seed of doubt in his mind that he was only in remission from the plasma, not cured of it. Dark Action Girl: Could hold her own against Fancy and then Dutch. Technically she even beat Dutch, or at least she succeeded in reaching the Plasma in Dutch's possession. It kind of helps that she used to be a Killjoy before she became Hullen.

characters.name: Weej
characters.description: Former Hullen soldier under Aneela's command who became one of the frozen 'dolls' after Kendry ordered them to stand down. Zeph has since 'reset' him. Creepy Monotone: His first words were "She is coming" said repeatedly. Later, it becomes "Something is wrong". The Comically Serious: Since Zeph only gave him limited capacities, he starts out as this. He gets better. Death Glare: Initially, this was all he was capable of. Defeat Means Friendship: In a sense. He was one of the many Hullen soldiers who froze. Zeph realized that his brainwaves kept repeating the same pattern, like he was glitching. She passed a low-level charge through his brain to interrupt the feedback loop and reset him. Since then, he's become Turin's confidant.

characters.name: The Lady
characters.description: A mysterious entity that lives inside the Green Plasma and appears to be the true ruler of the Hullen. A Form You Are Comfortable With: Inverted. She takes the forms of Aneela, Khlyen and Pree when finally confronted by Dutch, though she assumes them less to make Dutch more comfortable and more to make her moreuncomfortable.We finally see her true form in the finale. Ambiguous Gender Identity: Both of her host bodies are female, and she is always referred to with feminine pronouns. However, she seems equally comfortable with taking male forms; she suggests a male as a replacement body when her current one starts failing, and wants to useJaqas her permanent host. Animal Motifs: Spiders. Specifically, giant green spiders formed from the Plasma. Ax-Crazy: She has a tendency to threaten, torture or kill people around her, including her own henchmen.

characters.name: Dr. Pim Jaegar
characters.description: Hoist by Her Own Petard: A non-death example. Dutch orders Jaegar's assistant to wipe her memories using the same technology she used on D'avin. Inciting Incident: If the Company hadn't agreed to put out the kill warrant she requested on D'avin, the events of the show would have turned outverydifferently. Laser-Guided Amnesia: She inflicted this on D'avin, removing his memories of the mind-control experiment. She gets her own memories wiped at the end of "Kiss Kiss, Bye Bye". Mad Scientist: What else do you call someone who creates technology that can force anyone to become a murderousManchurian Agent? Granted, D'avin killing his squad was apparently not the intended result, but still.

characters.name: Clara/Olli
characters.description: Clara is a "Hackmod" (cybernetically augmented human) enslaved by a gang of Westerlyn bandits. She escaped with the help of the Killjoys, becoming an occasional ally. Oleana, nicknamed "Olli", is a hackmod who met John Jaqobis while he was tracking the whereabouts of Clara. Olli doesn't know when or how, but she woke up one day with Clara's cybernetic arm where her own used to be. Artificial Limbs: She has two interchangeable right arms, one that looks like a normal arm and one with a weapons system integrated into it. Badass Adorable: Her enhancements enable her to kick a large amount of ass and still look likeStephanie Leonidas. Olli doesn't look too bad, either. Cyborg: More than 26% of her is cybernetic. Legally speaking, she isn't considered a human being anymore. I Call It "Vera": Her right arm is a weapons system guided by an AI she calls "Alice."

characters.name: Jaqobis Kin Rit
characters.description: The biological child of Aneela Kin Rit and D'avin Jaqobis, with Delle Seyah Kendry as his surrogate mother. Affectionate Nickname: "Jaq," short for Jaqobis. Aneela also gives him the nickname "little bear." Big Eater: As a side effect ofhis rapid growth from a baby to a teenager in a single dayJaq has a voracious appetite. Half-Human Hybrid: He is a Hullen/Human hybrid. Healing Factor: Like all Hullen, he has one, but his is slower since he's partially human.
